title: Coelorachis helferi

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Coelorachis helferi include sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory problems and promote overall well-being.

This species is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as India and Sri Lanka. Coelorachis helferi is further classified as a monocot plant, characterized by one cotyledon during germination. It is closely related to other species within the same genus, such as Coelorachis caryotantha and Coelorachis squarrosa.
